title: Effect Command Semantics, Compatibility Modes, and Effect Help
description:
Implement the effect-command semantics feature set from the PRD at `tasks/prd-effect-command-semantics-compatibility-modes-and-effect-help.md`.

Scope:
- define a central typed effect model and registry in `tracker-core`
- route playback and import through the typed interpretation layer
- add project-level effect modes for `Riffl native` and `Compatible mode`
- preserve compatibility semantics during import where supported
- surface effect meaning in the editor status bar and a dedicated `Shift+K` help view

Completion for this epic means all child stories are finished and the PRD goals are covered end to end.

title: US-001: Define a central typed effect model and registry
description: As a Riffl developer, I want one central typed effect model and registry so that importers, playback, editor help, and future docs all use the same effect definitions.
acceptance_criteria:
- A central effect registry exists in `tracker-core` for Riffl-native effect definitions.
- The registry defines effect identity, parameters, continuation behavior, metadata, and help text.
- The internal runtime/editor model is typed rather than relying only on raw numeric interpretation.
- The registry is usable by import, playback, status-bar help, and detailed help view code.
- Public APIs added for this model are documented.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: US-002: Fix playback interpretation of effect commands
description: As a user, I want playback to interpret effect commands consistently so that imported and edited patterns sound correct.
acceptance_criteria:
- Playback no longer misinterprets continuation-style effects such as `300` as unrelated commands like speed `0`.
- Continuation behavior is handled according to effect definition rather than ad hoc numeric checks.
- Existing supported effect commands execute through the typed interpretation layer.
- Unsupported effects do not crash playback.
- Tests cover at least one continuation case and one non-continuation case.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: US-003: Add project-level effect modes
description: As a user, I want each project to choose between `Riffl native` and `Compatible mode` so that I can prioritize either Riffl semantics or source-tracker fidelity.
acceptance_criteria:
- Projects can store and load an effect mode setting.
- Supported modes are `Riffl native` and `Compatible mode`.
- Mode selection is project-level, not per pattern.
- Existing projects load with a documented default mode.
- Mode choice affects import and interpretation behavior in a deterministic way.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: Fix Config Loading/Saving Bug
description:
The application configuration system has a critical bug where user settings in config.toml are being overwritten with defaults. The issue occurs in `app.rs:285-336` where `Config::load()` is called to read user configuration, but then `self.config` is set to `Config::default()` instead of storing the loaded config. This causes any save operation (like toggling bookmarks) to write default values back to config.toml, erasing user preferences.

Additionally, hardcoded values ignore config defaults:
- Theme is hardcoded to `Nord` instead of using `config.theme_kind()`
- Pattern dimensions use hardcoded 16 rows, 4 channels instead of `config.default_pattern_rows` and `config.default_channels`
- BPM uses hardcoded 125.0 instead of `config.default_bpm`

## Root Cause
1. In `App::new()`, config is loaded but discarded
2. In `main.rs`, config is reassigned after `App::new()`, causing initialization with wrong defaults
3. Theme and other defaults ignore configuration

## Impact
- User configuration is lost on every save
- Theme preferences don't persist
- Custom defaults for BPM/rows/channels are ignored

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User configuration in config.toml persists across app restarts
- [ ] Config.toml is not overwritten with default values when bookmarks are toggled
- [ ] Theme preference from config is applied at startup
- [ ] Default BPM from config is used for new songs
- [ ] Default pattern rows/channels from config are used for new patterns
- [ ] Fix is minimal and doesn't break existing functionality

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes

title: US-004: Support compatible import semantics
description: As a user importing tracker files, I want `Compatible mode` to preserve source effect meaning so that playback matches the imported module as closely as possible.
acceptance_criteria:
- Importers translate source effects into Riffl's typed model while preserving source semantics where supported.
- If a source format defines a code as continuation, imported data preserves that continuation meaning in `Compatible mode`.
- Compatibility-only effects can exist when no exact Riffl-native effect exists.
- Compatibility effects execute according to source-format semantics when supported.
- Tests cover at least one imported effect whose source semantics differ from naive numeric interpretation.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: UI & Workflow Improvements
description: Collection of UI enhancements and workflow improvements including footer redesign, separate sample/instrument editors, improved file browsing, dashboard start window, and advanced sampling features.

title: Fix VU Meter Not Resetting When Music Pauses
description:
Currently, VU meters continue showing peak levels after music playback pauses or stops, which is confusing. The VU meters should reset to zero when playback stops/pauses to accurately reflect audio state.

## Problem Analysis
The VU meter rendering in `ui/vu_meters.rs` uses peak levels from the mixer, but these levels are not cleared when transport stops. The mixer's peak detection likely holds values until new audio is processed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VU meters reset to zero (empty bars) when transport stops (Space key)
- [ ] VU meters reset to zero when transport pauses 
- [ ] VU meters remain at zero until playback resumes
- [ ] Reset happens immediately, not gradually
- [ ] Works in both Pattern and Song playback modes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: Footer Layout Redesign (Left/Right Sections)
description:
The current footer mixes help hints, status indicators, and system info in a single left-aligned line, making it cluttered. Redesign to use left/right sections like Neovim plugins, with configurable elements.

## Problem Analysis
Current footer in `ui/mod.rs` `render_footer()` puts everything in one line. We need to split into left section (mode, position, selection) and right section (system info, help hint, view indicator). Configurable via `StatusBarConfig`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Split footer into left-aligned and right-aligned sections
- [ ] Left section: mode indicator, octave, instrument, step size, position, pattern, selection
- [ ] Right section: help hint (?), view indicator, CPU, memory, follow/draw mode indicators
- [ ] Maintain all existing information but better organized
- [ ] Respect existing `StatusBarConfig` toggle settings
- [ ] Ensure proper alignment and spacing for various terminal widths
- [ ] Update documentation in `config.rs` about status bar layout
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: Separate Sample Editor from Instrument Editor
description:
Currently, sample editing is embedded within the instrument editor view. Create a dedicated sample editor with waveform display, loop points, and basic editing separate from instrument parameters.

## Problem Analysis
The instrument editor in `instrument_editor.rs` mixes sample editing with instrument parameters. Sample-specific features (trim, reverse, normalize) belong in a separate editor. This separation follows DAW conventions and improves workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create new `sample_editor.rs` module with dedicated UI
- [ ] Move waveform display from instrument editor to sample editor
- [ ] Sample editor shows: waveform visualization, loop points, sample metadata
- [ ] Sample editor has basic tools: zoom, cursor navigation, selection
- [ ] Instrument editor focuses on: volume, tuning, envelopes, LFOs, keyzones
- [ ] Update instrument list view to allow opening sample editor for selected instrument
- [ ] Ensure backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved
- [ ] Update help text and documentation
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: Improved Loading Window with Search (/) and DAW Features
description:
The current file browser is basic. Enhance with search (press `/` to focus search), filtering, preview, and features common in other DAWs like recent files, favorites, and better navigation.

## Problem Analysis
Current `file_browser.rs` and `sample_browser.rs` lack search functionality and advanced features. Users need `/` to search, filtering by extension, preview of audio files, and better keyboard navigation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add search functionality triggered by `/` key in file browser
- [ ] Real-time filtering of files as user types
- [ ] Support regex or wildcard patterns in search
- [ ] Add recent files list (last 10 opened)
- [ ] Add favorites/bookmarks system
- [ ] File preview: show basic info for selected audio files (duration, format, size)
- [ ] Improved keyboard navigation (fuzzy matching, quick jump)
- [ ] Maintain backward compatibility with existing file browser API
- [ ] Update help documentation
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: Advanced File Browsing Integration (yazi)
description:
Optional integration with external file managers like yazi for advanced browsing capabilities, or implement similar features natively if yazi integration is complex.

## Problem Analysis
Users want "browse freely" with features found in modern file managers. Two approaches: 1) Integrate with yazi (external TUI file manager) via subprocess, or 2) Implement similar features natively (dual-pane, thumbnails, advanced sorting).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Research yazi integration feasibility (subprocess, piping file selections)
- [ ] If feasible: implement yazi integration mode in file browser
- [ ] If not feasible: implement native dual-pane browser
- [ ] Native features: dual-pane view, advanced sorting (name, date, size, type)
- [ ] Thumbnail/preview for common audio formats
- [ ] Batch operations (select multiple files)
- [ ] Configurable to use yazi or native browser
- [ ] Maintain existing simple browser as fallback
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: Dashboard/Start Window for Project Selection
description:
Add an initial dashboard window that appears on startup, allowing users to create new project, load existing project/MOD file, open recent projects, or access tutorials.

## Problem Analysis
Currently app jumps straight into pattern editor. A start window improves UX, especially for new users, and matches modern DAW/editor conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create new `dashboard.rs` module with start screen
- [ ] Options: New Project, Load Project, Load MOD/IT/XM/S3M, Recent Files, Tutorial, Quit
- [ ] Recent files list (last 5-10 projects)
- [ ] Visual design matching Riffl theme
- [ ] Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter to select)
- [ ] Configurable to skip dashboard on startup (for power users)
- [ ] Integrate with project loading/saving system
- [ ] Update main.rs to show dashboard before main app
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: Advanced Sampling Features (Trim/Reverse/Normalize)
description:
Add professional sampling features to the sample editor: trim silence, reverse, normalize, fade in/out, time stretch, and basic effects for flexible sample manipulation.

## Problem Analysis
Users want "a lot of flexibilities" to modify samples. Current sample support is basic playback. Need DSP operations for sample editing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Trim: remove silence from start/end of sample
- [ ] Reverse: reverse sample playback
- [ ] Normalize: adjust gain to maximum without clipping
- [ ] Fade in/out: apply linear or exponential fade curves
- [ ] Time stretch: basic pitch-preserving time adjustment
- [ ] DC offset removal
- [ ] Basic effects: bit crusher, sample rate reduction
- [ ] Non-destructive editing (preserve original)
- [ ] Undo/redo for sample edits
- [ ] Batch apply to multiple samples
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo test passes
- [ ] Quality Gate: cargo clippy passes

title: US-005: Define native import conversion behavior
description: As a user importing into `Riffl native` mode, I want source effects converted into Riffl-native effects so that the project behaves as a native Riffl composition even if round-trip fidelity is lost.
acceptance_criteria:
- In `Riffl native` mode, import converts supported external effects to native Riffl effects.
- When conversion changes behavior from the source format, that change is documented in the effect metadata/help surface.
- Exact source-faithful behavior is not required in native mode.
- Import conversion rules are deterministic and test-covered.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: US-006: Handle non-exact native mappings safely
description: As a user, I want effects without an exact native equivalent to degrade predictably so that I understand when behavior is approximate.
acceptance_criteria:
- In `Compatible mode`, effects without an exact Riffl-native equivalent may remain as compatibility-only effects.
- In `Riffl native` mode, import converts to the nearest native effect only when an explicit mapping exists.
- Approximate native conversions are labeled clearly as approximate in UI/help.
- If no explicit native mapping exists, the system preserves enough information to show the user what was imported.
- Unknown or unsupported values are preserved as raw data rather than discarded.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: US-007: Show effect meaning in the status bar
description: As a pattern editor user, I want to see the meaning of the selected effect cell in the status bar so that I can understand commands while navigating.
acceptance_criteria:
- When the cursor is on an effect cell, the status bar shows the effect label, parsed parameters, and a short explanation of playback behavior.
- The summary is generated from the same central effect registry and interpretation layer used by playback and import.
- Compatibility-only and approximate-conversion cases are labeled clearly.
- Unknown effects show a clear fallback message instead of blank or misleading text.
- Moving the cursor updates the displayed effect meaning.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: Fix NSF Playback Issues
description:
NSF (NES Sound Format) files load but audio generation fails or produces incorrect output. The NSF loader uses the `game-music-emu` crate but may have integration issues with the mixer or incorrect handling of NES APU emulation.

## Problem Analysis
1. **NSF loader**: `tracker-core/src/format/nsf.rs` implements `ModuleLoader` using `game-music-emu`
2. **NES APU channels**: NSF files use 2A03/2A07 APU with 5 channels (2 pulse, triangle, noise, DMC)
3. **Mixer integration**: NSF audio output may not be properly routed to the mixer
4. **Timing issues**: NES timing (NTSC vs PAL) may not match tracker timing
5. **Instrument mapping**: NSF instruments may not map correctly to tracker instrument system

## Symptoms
- NSF files load but produce no sound or distorted audio
- Multiple tracks/channels may not play correctly
- Timing may be off (too fast/slow)
- Audio may cut out or have glitches

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] NSF files produce correct audio output
- [ ] All NES APU channels work (pulse1, pulse2, triangle, noise, DMC)
- [ ] Multiple songs in NSF files can be selected and played
- [ ] Timing is correct (NTSC 60Hz, PAL 50Hz as appropriate)
- [ ] Volume and panning are preserved
- [ ] Basic tracker playback controls work (play, stop, pattern/song mode)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- At least 2 test NSF files play with correct audio output

title: US-008: Add detailed effect help via Shift+K
description: As a user, I want to press `Shift+K` on an effect cell to open a dedicated help view so that I can inspect the command in more detail.
acceptance_criteria:
- `Shift+K` from an effect cell opens a dedicated effect-help view or page.
- The help view shows effect name, parsed parameters, and a human-readable description of playback behavior.
- The help view indicates whether the effect is native, compatibility-only, unknown, or an approximate conversion.
- The help content is sourced from the same central effect metadata as the status bar.
- The view can be exited cleanly back to the editor.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.

title: Phase 2: Renoise-Level Instruments & UI Overhaul
description:
Phase 2 focuses on two major goals:
1. Renoise-Level Instrument System - Multi-samples, keyzones, envelopes, LFOs
2. TUI Ergonomic Overhaul - Visualizers, command bar, which-key menu, themes

This epic tracks the Phase 2 implementation as defined in the master PRD.

title: US5.2: Unified Loader Architecture
description:
As a user, I want a unified loader architecture that handles all supported formats consistently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single code path for loading all tracker formats (XM, IT, S3M, MOD)
- [ ] Shared error handling and validation
- [ ] Format-specific quirks handled in dedicated handlers
- [ ] Clear separation between format detection and parsing
- [ ] cargo test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: Theme System (Gruvbox, Nord, Solarized)

## Acceptance Criteria

title: LFOs (Pitch, Volume, Panning)

As a user, I want LFOs for modulation of pitch, volume, and panning.

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Fix VU Meter Alignment
description:
The VU meter visualization introduced in the UI overhaul has broken alignment with channel headers. The VU meter bars and oscilloscope waveforms do not align properly with their corresponding channel columns in the pattern editor.

## Problem Analysis
The issue appears to be in column width calculations:
1. In `ui/mod.rs:534-579`, VU meters and oscilloscopes are rendered in separate rows
2. In `vu_meters.rs:66-105`, column widths are calculated using `Constraint::Ratio(1, num_channels)`
3. Channel headers in `ui/mod.rs:599-629` may use different width calculations
4. Scroll position (`ch_scroll`) may not be accounted for consistently
5. Odd/even width handling may cause misalignment (e.g., `col_area.width / 2` in vu_meters.rs:88)

## Impact
- Visual misalignment breaks the professional appearance
- Users cannot accurately associate VU levels with specific channels
- Oscilloscope waveforms appear shifted relative to their channels

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VU meter bars align precisely with channel header columns
- [ ] Oscilloscope waveforms align with their corresponding channels
- [ ] Alignment works correctly with any number of visible channels (1-64)
- [ ] Horizontal scrolling doesn't break alignment
- [ ] Odd channel widths are handled correctly (no visual artifacts)
- [ ] Column width calculations are consistent between VU meters and channel headers

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- Visual inspection shows perfect column alignment in the UI

title: Fix Remaining XM/IT/MOD Accuracy Issues
description:
Issue riffl-9b3.4 (Accuracy Audit for XM/IT) was marked closed but significant playback accuracy problems remain across XM, IT, and MOD formats. These include sample loading issues, speed/tempo problems, and effect command interpretation errors.

## Specific Problems Identified
1. **Sample loading issues**: Some XM/IT samples don't load or play correctly
2. **Speed/tempo problems**: MOD/IT speed settings not working correctly
3. **Effect command interpretation**: 
   - `3xx` (tone portamento) continuation behavior incorrect (e.g., `300` treated as speed 0 instead of continuing previous portamento)
   - Other continuation-style effects mis-handled
4. **Instrument/sample mapping**: Multi-sample instruments may not work correctly
5. **MOD compatibility**: ProTracker behavior differences

## Relation to riffl-9b3.4
This issue addresses problems that were NOT resolved by the initial accuracy audit. The audit identified issues but some fixes were incomplete or new issues emerged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] XM files play with correct sample interpolation (linear/nearest)
- [ ] IT files handle speed/tempo correctly (separate speed and tempo tracks)
- [ ] MOD files play with ProTracker compatibility mode
- [ ] Effect command `300` behaves as tone portamento continue (not speed 0)
- [ ] Continuation effects work correctly across pattern breaks
- [ ] Sample loops (forward, ping-pong) work correctly
- [ ] Volume/panning envelopes work for XM/IT instruments
- [ ] At least 5 test modules per format play correctly (reference: OpenMPT, MilkyTracker, SchismTracker)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- Existing tests continue to pass (no regression)

title: Overhaul Follow Mode Behavior
description:
The current follow mode implementation moves the editor cursor with playback, which is counter-intuitive and breaks editing workflow. Follow mode should allow viewing playback position without disrupting cursor placement, and should provide horizontal view scrolling controls.

## Current Problem
- In follow mode, cursor moves with playback, preventing editing during playback
- No way to scroll horizontal view independently during playback
- Track widths cannot be adjusted to see more/less channels

## Expected Behavior
1. **Cursor stays fixed**: During playback in follow mode, editor cursor remains at user-selected position
2. **Horizontal view scrolling**: `h` and `l` keys scroll the horizontal view (not move cursor) when in follow mode
3. **Track width adjustment**: `-` and `+` (or similar) adjust visible track width/zoom level
4. **Visual indicator**: Clear UI indication when follow mode is active with special behavior

## Use Cases
- User wants to edit pattern ahead of playback position while following playback
- User wants to view different channels during playback without losing cursor position
- User wants to zoom in/out on tracks during playback for detailed monitoring

## Implementation Notes
- Modify `app.rs` follow mode logic to not update cursor position during playback
- Add horizontal scroll controls that work in follow mode (separate from cursor movement)
- Add track width adjustment controls
- Update UI to show follow mode state with different cursor/scroll behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cursor position remains fixed during playback when follow mode is enabled
- [ ] `h` key scrolls view left (not move cursor) in follow mode
- [ ] `l` key scrolls view right (not move cursor) in follow mode
- [ ] Track width can be adjusted with keyboard shortcuts (e.g., `-`/`+` or `[`/`]`)
- [ ] UI clearly indicates follow mode is active with special behavior
- [ ] Normal cursor movement works when playback is stopped or follow mode is off
- [ ] Horizontal scroll position is preserved when toggling follow mode

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- No regression in existing follow mode functionality (playback position still visible)

title: Show Pattern Position and Pattern Number
description:
The current display shows only a pattern number, but in arrangement/song mode this number actually represents the position in the arrangement, not the pattern index. This causes confusion between arrangement position and pattern number.

## Problem
- Status bar shows "Pattern: XX" but in song mode this is actually the arrangement position
- No visual distinction between position in arrangement and pattern index
- Users cannot tell if they're looking at pattern 5 at position 3, or pattern 3 at position 5

## Solution
Display both arrangement position and pattern number clearly, e.g.:
- "Pos: 03 / Pat: 05" (position 3, pattern 5)
- Or "03→05" (position 3, pattern 5)
- In pattern mode: "Pat: 05" (no position)
- In song mode: "Pos: 03 → Pat: 05"

## Implementation Areas
1. **Status bar**: Update pattern display in footer
2. **Arrangement view**: Show both numbers in arrangement editor
3. **Pattern editor header**: Indicate current position/pattern
4. **Playback display**: Update in real-time during playback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status bar shows both arrangement position and pattern number in song mode
- [ ] Status bar shows only pattern number in pattern mode
- [ ] Arrangement view clearly displays position→pattern mapping
- [ ] Pattern editor header shows current position/pattern information
- [ ] Display updates in real-time during playback
- [ ] Format is clear and unambiguous (e.g., "Pos: 03 / Pat: 05")
- [ ] Works with pattern/song playback mode switching

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- No regression in existing pattern/arrangement functionality

title: US4.2: Envelope Editor

- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: Command Bar (colon commands)
description:
As a user, I want a command bar for rapid operations like :load, :save, :bpm.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Colon key opens command bar
- [ ] Commands: :load, :save, :bpm, :volume, :quit
- [ ] Command history (up/down arrows)
- [ ] Tab completion for commands
- [ ] Error messages for invalid commands
- [ ] cargo test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: Fix S3M Playback (Silent)
description:
S3M (ScreamTracker 3) files load without errors but produce no audio output. The S3M loader implementation exists in `tracker-core/src/format/s3m.rs` but appears to have issues with either sample loading, Adlib/OPL synthesis, or mixer integration.

## Problem Analysis
1. **S3M loader structure**: The `S3mLoader` implements `ModuleLoader` but may have parsing issues
2. **Adlib/OPL synthesis**: S3M files often use OPL instruments which are handled in `tracker-core/src/audio/adlib.rs`
3. **Sample loading**: S3M samples are 8-bit unsigned PCM with possible compression/looping issues
4. **Effect commands**: S3M-specific effects may not be interpreted correctly
5. **Mixer integration**: Loaded instruments may not be properly connected to the audio engine

## Symptoms
- S3M files appear to load successfully (no error messages)
- Pattern data is visible in the editor
- Transport shows playback but no audio output
- VU meters show no activity during playback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] S3M files produce audible output when played
- [ ] PCM samples load correctly and play at correct pitch
- [ ] Adlib/OPL instruments produce synthesized sound
- [ ] Basic S3M effect commands (arpeggio, vibrato, volume slide) work
- [ ] Stereo panning is preserved (S3M supports stereo samples)
- [ ] Looping samples work correctly (forward and ping-pong)
- [ ] Module tempo and speed settings are respected

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- At least 3 test S3M files play with correct audio output

title: Add Settings UI Menu
description:
Currently, configuration is only possible by editing config.toml directly. Users need an in-app settings menu to adjust preferences without manually editing configuration files.

## Problem
- Users must edit config.toml in a text editor to change settings
- No way to preview theme changes before applying
- Configuration errors can break the application
- No discovery of available settings

## Solution
Create a settings UI menu accessible via `:settings` command or dedicated keybinding (`Ctrl+,` or similar). The menu should allow:
1. Theme selection with live preview
2. Default settings configuration (BPM, pattern rows, channels)
3. Audio settings (sample rate, buffer size)
4. Keybinding customization (stretch goal)
5. Save/apply/cancel functionality

## Implementation Approach
1. **New modal dialog**: Settings modal similar to export/help dialogs
2. **Tabbed interface**: Categories for themes, defaults, audio, etc.
3. **Live preview**: Theme changes preview immediately
4. **Config integration**: Changes save to config.toml via existing `Config::save()`
5. **Validation**: Prevent invalid settings

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Settings menu accessible via `:settings` command
- [ ] Theme selection with preview of available themes (dark, mocha, nord, etc.)
- [ ] Default BPM configuration (range: 30-300)
- [ ] Default pattern rows configuration (range: 1-256)
- [ ] Default channels configuration (range: 1-64)
- [ ] Settings save to config.toml when confirmed
- [ ] Changes apply immediately or on restart as appropriate
- [ ] Cancel button discards changes
- [ ] Menu has clear section headings and descriptions

## Stretch Goals
- [ ] Keybinding customization interface
- [ ] Audio device selection
- [ ] Sample directory management
- [ ] Export default settings

## Quality Gates
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` passes
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- No regression in existing config loading/saving
